items.title: Argentine MPs approve Milei-backed bill to allow mining in glaciers
items.summary: Argentina's lower house of parliament on Thursday approved a bill backed by President Javier Milei to permit mining in glacier regions. Environmentalists and ​scientists have ‌warned that mining in in frozen parts of the Andes mountains would threaten water resources. The law takes effect once it is published in ‌the official gazette.

items.title: Pakistan plays major role trying to mediate an end to Iran war
items.summary: Pakistan has emerged as a key mediator between the US and Iran, playing a crucial role in brokering the two-week ceasefire agreement and working with both camps, as well as regional powers, to end the war. Islamabad has ties to Tehran and Washington and is set to hold talks this weekend.

items.title: Netanyahu fails to address Israel’s failure to achieve war goals in Iran
items.summary: Reporting from Jerusalem, FRANCE 24’s Noga Tarnopolsky says that when Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu finally made a statement on the US-Iran ceasefire, he failed to “address the fact that Israel has not achieved any of its stated war goals, which included regime change in Iran, the destruction of Iran's ability to launch and to produce ballistic missiles” and eliminate the nuclear threat.

items.title: US-Iran ceasefire: Why Tehran wants to charge ships for crossing the Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: As part of its 10-point proposal to end the US-Israeli war on Iran, Tehran is pushing for ongoing control over the vital Strait of Hormuz – including charging what officials suggest could be as much as $2 million per ship to help the country rebuild its bombed-out infrastructure and maintain control over one of the world's most strategic waterways.

items.title: PSG take control of Champions League semi-finals tie with win against Liverpool
items.summary: PSG took a big step towards the Champions League semi-finals with a 2-0 win over Liverpool at home in the first leg. Désiré Doué opened the scoring in the 11th minute, before Khvicha Kvaratskhelia doubled the lead in the 65th minute. The reigning champions could even have regrets, given how much they dominated the match (74 percent possession, 18 shots).

items.title: Middle East live: France blasts Israel’s ‘unacceptable’ Lebanon strikes, ceasefire hangs in balance
items.summary: French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot on Thursday condemned Israel’s "unacceptable" strikes on Lebanon, which killed 182 people and wounded 890 the day after the US and Iran reached a two-week ceasefire agreement. As Iran and Hezbollah retaliated for the attacks, Barrot warned the Israeli strikes could undermine the fragile ceasefire. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

items.title: Iran-US truce on brink as Israel pounds Lebanon, Tehran warns of escalation
items.summary: A fragile two-week truce between Iran and the United States hung in the balance on Wednesday as Tehran threatened renewed hostilities and Israel launched heavy strikes on Lebanon. Both sides claimed victory, but at least 182 people were killed and nearly 900 wounded, Lebanese authorities said.

items.title: CAF’s Motsepe welcomes corruption probe after Senegal dispute
items.summary: Confederation of African Football (CAF) president Patrice Motsepe on Wednesday said he would welcome a corruption investigation, adding the body had nothing to hide after meeting Senegalese officials in Dakar. Senegal last month called for a probe after CAF stripped it of the 2025 AFCON title and awarded it to Morocco.

items.title: Trump meets NATO’s Rutte as US weighs exit over Iran war
items.summary: US President Donald Trump held talks with NATO chief Mark Rutte on Wednesday, the White House said, as he weighed leaving the alliance after it did not join the US in its war against Iran. The Wall Street Journal reported that Trump was also considering withdrawing US troops from some NATO countries.

items.title: At least 182 killed as Israel strikes central Beirut after saying Iran truce doesn't apply there
items.summary: Lebanon’s health ministry said that Israeli strikes on Wednesday killed 182 people, the highest single-day death toll in the Israel-Hezbollah war. Israel launched a barrage of strikes in central Beirut and elsewhere in the country as a shaky ceasefire took effect between the U.S. and Iran. Iranian officials have maintained that the deal was supposed to include Lebanon, while Israel and the U.S. have insisted that it does not. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Sami Nader, Director of the Lev

items.title: Pour l'archevêque d'Alger, le pape Léon XIV vient "rencontrer le peuple algérien"
items.summary: Quelques jours avant la visite de Léon XIV en Algérie, prévue du 13 au 15 avril, l'archevêque,  cardinal d'Alger, a accordé un entretien à France 24. Mgr Jean-Paul Vesco a personnellement invité le pape dès son élection. Après des voyages en Turquie et au Liban, le souverain pontife veut, selon lui, continuer "à construire des ponts entre le monde musulman et chrétien".

translation.title: For the Archbishop of Algiers, Pope Leo XIV comes "to meet the Algerian people"
translation.summary: A few days before Leo XIV's visit to Algeria, scheduled for April 13-15, the Archbishop, Cardinal of Algiers, granted an interview to France 24. Bishop Jean-Paul Vesco personally invited the pope as soon as he was elected. After traveling to Turkey and Lebanon, the sovereign pontiff, according to him, wants to continue "building bridges between the Muslim and Christian world".

items.title: L'Argentine réforme des zones protégées autour des glaciers pour promouvoir l'exploitation minière
items.summary: Le Congrès argentin a adopté dans la nuit de mercredi à jeudi une réforme permettant un redécoupage des zones protégées autour des glaciers du pays. Une manière de faciliter l'activité minière dans ces lieux sensibles, et un recul environnemental dénoncé par les écologistes.

translation.title: Argentina is reforming glacier protected areas to promote mining
translation.summary: In the night of Wednesday to Thursday, the Argentine Congress adopted a reform allowing the redistribution of protected areas around the country's glaciers. A way to facilitate mining activity in these sensitive places, and an environmental decline denounced by environmentalists.

items.title: "Cessez-le-feu" entre Washington et Téhéran : "Les uns signent, le Liban saigne"
items.summary: A la Une de la presse, ce jeudi 9 avril, le lendemain de l’annonce du "cessez-le-feu" entre les Etats-Unis et l’Iran, qui estime que des négociations n’ont "aucun sens" au vu des violations observées hier au Liban, où les frappes israéliennes ont fait des centaines de victimes. La trêve conclue entre Téhéran et Washington est aussi accueillie avec scepticisme ailleurs dans le monde, y compris aux Etats-Unis. Et la mission Artemis 2, objet de thèses conspirationnistes plus ou moins inventives.

translation.title: "Cease fire" between Washington and Tehran: "Some sign, Lebanon bleeds"
translation.summary: On the front page of the press, on Thursday 9 April, the day after the announcement of the "cease fire" between the United States and Iran, which believes that negotiations have "no meaning" in view of the violations observed yesterday in Lebanon, where the Israeli strikes claimed hundreds of victims. The truce between Tehran and Washington is also welcomed with skepticism elsewhere in the world, including in the United States. And the mission Artemis 2, object of conspiracy theses more or less inventive.

items.title: Le PSG s’impose face à Liverpool et prend une belle option pour les demi-finales
items.summary: Le PSG a fait un grand pas vers les demi-finales de la Ligue des champions en battant Liverpool à domicile lors du match aller (2-0). Désiré Doué a ouvert le score dès la 11e minute, avant que Khvicha Kvaratskhelia ne double la mise à la 65e minute. Les Parisiens peuvent même nourrir des regrets tant ils ont dominé la rencontre (74 % de possession, 18 tirs).

translation.title: PSG wins Liverpool and takes a great option for the semifinals
translation.summary: The PSG took a big step towards the semifinals of the Champions League by beating Liverpool at home in the game-go (2-0). Desired Doué opened the score in the 11th minute, before Khvicha Kvaratskhelia doubled the bet in the 65th minute. Parisians can even feed regrets as they dominated the meeting (74% possession, 18 shots).

items.title: À Dakar, le président de la CAF promet un football africain débarrassé de tous ses "problèmes"
items.summary: Trois semaines après la décision contestée d'un jury d'appel de la Confédération africaine de football de retirer au Sénégal son titre de champion d'Afrique, le président de la CAF Patrice Motsepe s'est rendu au Sénégal mercredi où il a promis des réformes pour que le football africain soit plus "respecté".

translation.title: In Dakar, the president of the CAF promises an African football without any "problems"
translation.summary: Three weeks after the contested decision of an appeal board of the African Football Confederation to withdraw from Senegal his title as African champion, the president of CAF Patrice Motsepe visited Senegal on Wednesday, where he promised reforms to make African football more "respected".

items.title: Donald Trump, très remonté contre l'Otan, a reçu Mark Rutte à la Maison Blanche
items.summary: Le président américain Donald Trump a reçu mercredi à Washington le patron de l'Otan Mark Rutte. La porte-parole de la Maison Blanche a affirmé qu'il était "assez triste que l'Otan ait tourné le dos aux Américains au cours des six dernières semaines" de guerre au Moyen-Orient. Selon le Wall Street Journal, les États-Unis songent à retirer des troupes aujourd'hui stationnées dans des pays qui n'ont pas soutenu l'offensive contre l'Iran.

translation.title: Donald Trump, very up against NATO, received Mark Rutte at the White House
translation.summary: US President Donald Trump received on Wednesday in Washington the boss of NATO Mark Rutte. The White House spokesman said it was "sad enough that NATO has turned its back on the Americans in the last six weeks" of war in the Middle East. According to the Wall Street Journal, the United States is considering withdrawing troops now stationed in countries that have not supported the offensive against Iran.

items.title: Moyen-Orient en direct : la France dénonce des "attaques intolérables" d'Israël au Liban
items.summary: Le Hezbollah pro-iranien a dit jeudi avoir lancé des roquettes sur Israël, en réaction à sa "violation du cessez-le-feu". La France a qualifié les frappes israéliennes d'"intolérables". De son côté,  Donald Trump assure que l'armée américaine restera déployée à proximité de l'Iran jusqu'à l'application complète d'un "réel accord". Suivez notre direct.

translation.title: Middle East live: France denounces Israel's "unbearable attacks" in Lebanon
translation.summary: Pro-Iranian Hezbollah said on Thursday that it had launched rockets at Israel in response to its "ceasefire violation". France called the Israeli strikes "intolerable". Donald Trump, for his part, assures that the US army will remain deployed near Iran until a "real deal" is fully implemented. Follow our direct.

items.title: Guerre au Moyen-Orient : Israël bombarde le Liban et fragilise la trêve avec l'Iran
items.summary: Israël a lancé mercredi au Liban sa "plus grande frappe coordonnée" contre le Hezbollah depuis le déclenchement de la guerre. Le dernier bilan fait état de 182 morts et 890 blessés. L'Iran, qui a accepté la veille un cessez-le-feu de 15 jours avec les États-Unis, menace de reprendre les hostilités.

translation.title: War in the Middle East: Israel bombards Lebanon and weakens the truce with Iran
translation.summary: Israel launched on Wednesday in Lebanon its "greatest coordinated strike" against Hezbollah since the outbreak of the war. The most recent report shows 182 deaths and 890 injuries. Iran, which had accepted a 15-day ceasefire with the United States the day before, threatened to resume hostilities.

items.title: Ligue des champions : Le PSG, tout en maîtrise, bat Liverpool
items.summary: Le Paris SG a dominé sans trembler Liverpool 2 à 0 en quart de finale aller de la Ligue des champions mercredi, prenant une belle option sur la qualification pour les demies. Le récit d'Eliott Samuel, envoyé spécial au Parc des Princes.

translation.title: Champions League: PSG, all in mastery, beat Liverpool
translation.summary: The Paris SG dominated with no trembling Liverpool 2 to 0 in the quarter final go from the Champions League on Wednesday, taking a nice option on qualifying for the half. The story of Eliott Samuel, special envoy to Princes Park.

items.title: Menaces de Trump sur l'Iran : le 25e amendement peut-il être activé pour inaptitude à exercer ?
items.summary: A 79 ans, Donald Trump essuie des accusations régulières de déclin physique et cognitif. Et ces dernières n'ont jamais été aussi fortes que depuis quelques jours, en raison de publications chocs du président américain sur son réseau Truth Social. Des démocrates, mais aussi d'anciens alliés du camp MAGA évoquent l'activation du 25ème amendement de la Constitution pour déclarer le chef de l'Etat inapte à exercer ses fonctions. En réalité, cette procédure a très peu de chances d'aboutir.

translation.title: Trump's threats to Iran: Can the 25th amendment be activated for incapacity to exercise?
translation.summary: At 79, Donald Trump was regularly charged with physical and cognitive decline. And these have never been as strong as in the last few days, due to the US President's shock publications on his Truth Social network. Democrats, as well as former allies of Camp MAGA, refer to the activation of the 25th amendment of the Constitution to declare the head of state unfit to perform his duties. In fact, this procedure is unlikely to succeed.

The Circular states that the Fourth National Agricultural Survey is aimed at rural households in the People ' s Republic of China, including rural agricultural producers and other households; urban agricultural producers; agricultural production units; villagers ' committees; and town and town people ' s governments. The main elements of the census include agricultural production conditions, food and food production, new quality of agricultural productivity, basics of rural development, living conditions of rural residents, etc. The standard time of the census is 2400 hours on 31 December 2026, and the data for the period is annual 2026. items.title: 全国人大常委会启动节约能源法执法检查
items.summary: 新华社北京6月5日电 为推动节约能源法全面有效实施，促进更高水平、更高质量做好节能工作，全国人大常委会5日启动节约能源法执法检查。 节约能源法自1998年1月1日实施，并于2007年、2016年、2018年进行了修改。 记者5日从全国人大常委会节约能源法执法检查组第一次全体会议上了解到，为全面深入了解法律实施情况，此次执法检查采取实地检查与委托检查相结合的方式，检查组将赴天津、江苏、湖南、广东、广西、四川等省（区、市）开展实地检查，并委托北京、河北、山西、吉林、上海、重庆等省（市）人大常委会对本行政区域内节约能源法实施情况进行检查。检查组将采取实地检查、听取汇报、组织座谈、典型调研、专题研究、抽查暗访等多种方式，全面准确了解法律实施情况，对带有普遍性的问题深入调查研究。 检查组将重点检查8方面内容：落实节能管理制度情况，推进重点领域节能工作情况，重点用能单位节能管理情况，推动节能技术进步情况，实施节能激励措施情况，与法律配套的行政法规、部门规章以及地方性法规的制定情况，法律实施中存在的其他问题，对节约能源法修改的意见和建议。

translation.title: The Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress initiated an energy-saving law enforcement inspection
translation.summary: In order to promote the full and effective implementation of the Energy Conservation Act and to promote higher and higher-quality energy efficiency, the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress launched an energy conservation law enforcement inspection on 5 June. The Energy Conservation Act has been in force since 1 January 1998 and was amended in 2007, 2016 and 2018. On 5 May, journalists learned from the first plenary meeting of the Standing Committee of the National People ' s Congress ' s Standing Committee on Energy Conservation Law Enforcement Inspection Unit that, in order to obtain a comprehensive and in-depth understanding of the implementation of the law, the inspection took the form of a combination of on-site inspections and commissioned inspections, which will be carried out in the provinces (districts and municipalities) of Tianjin, Jiangsu, Hunan, Guangdong, Guangxi, Sichuan and Beijing, Hebei, Shanxi, Jilin, Shanghai and Chongqing, among others. The Unit will have a comprehensive and accurate understanding of the implementation of the law and will conduct in-depth research and studies on issues of a general nature, in a variety of ways, including on-site inspections, debriefings, organization of talks, typical research, thematic studies, spot-checking and unannounced visits. The Unit will focus on eight areas: implementation of energy-saving management systems; promotion of energy-saving efforts in priority areas; focus on energy-saving management in energy-use units; promotion of technological progress in energy-saving; implementation of energy-saving incentives; development of administrative, sectoral and local regulations in conjunction with the law; other issues in the implementation of the law; and suggestions and recommendations for changes to the energy-saving law.

items.title: Russian man who joked his way onto ‘foreign agent’ list gets suspended sentence
items.summary: A Russian court has sentenced Marat Nikandrov, a Moscow Region resident who in 2023 was designated as a foreign agent by Russian authorities on the basis of his own application, to eight months on a suspended sentence, the Russian business news outlet RBC reported, citing the court ruling. He was also given a one-year probationary period.

items.title: Report: Kaliningrad cancels Victory Day military parade

items.title: A Russian children’s dance group won a competition after performing a routine set to Putin’s speech announcing the war against Ukraine
items.summary: At the end of March, a theater in Sochi hosted the Crystal Magnolia festival for young performers. The grand prize went to Evelia, a children’s dance studio from Shebekino — a border town in Russia’s Belgorod region that has been heavily affected by the war against Ukraine.

items.title: Bloomberg: Russia’s weekly oil export revenue reaches highest level since June 2022
items.summary: Russia’s oil export revenue has climbed to its highest level since the early weeks of the full-scale war in Ukraine, driven by rising prices and higher shipment volumes, the Bloomberg news agency reported on April 8.

items.title: Sabarimala case: Centre backs curbs on women’s entry, says 2018 verdict assumed 'men superior'
items.summary: The Centre has told the Supreme Court that the Sabarimala temple's ban on women of menstruating age should be upheld, arguing the 2018 verdict was flawed. Solicitor General Tushar Mehta contended that religious practices aren't solely about gender and cited diverse traditions. The court is examining how equality principles intersect with religious freedom.

items.title: IPL 2026: Shubman Gill punished despite GT’s last-ball thriller win over DC
items.summary: Shubman Gill's Gujarat Titans clinched a thrilling one-run victory against Delhi Capitals in IPL 2026. The captain's tactical decisions, including a crucial gamble on the final ball, ultimately secured the hard-fought win.

items.title: Was Lebanon part of ceasefire deal or not? How Pakistan may have muddled US-Iran truce

items.title: Anthropic CEO says AI will take your job;but his own company find enough people to work
